journal_entry.description: Acquisition of offshore marine structure with asset retirement obligation
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS (IAS 16 and IFRIC 1), when an entity acquires an asset with a legal or constructive obligation to restore the site, the present value of the restoration cost must be capitalized as part of the asset cost and a corresponding liability recognized. The marine structure is recorded at $4,000,000 (purchase price) plus $1,950,000 (present value of restoration obligation) = $5,950,000 total asset cost. The structure itself and the restoration obligation are recorded separately to reflect their different nature: the structure is a depreciable asset, while the restoration obligation is a provision that will accrete over time. Accounts Payable is credited for the $4,000,000 purchase on account, and Asset Retirement Obligation liability is credited for the $1,950,000 present value of future restoration costs. This treatment aligns with the debit tuple (2 asset increases) and credit tuple (2 liability increases).

output.reason: Under IFRS, the offshore marine structure is capitalized as an asset increase (Property, Plant & Equipment). The present value of the restoration obligation ($1,950,000) is added to the cost of the asset per IAS 16 and IFRIC 1, creating a second asset increase for the decommissioning/restoration component. Total: 2 distinct asset increases on the debit side - one for the structure acquisition ($4,000,000) and one for the capitalized restoration cost ($1,950,000).

debit_classifier.reason: Under IFRS, the offshore marine structure is capitalized as an asset increase (Property, Plant & Equipment). The present value of the restoration obligation ($1,950,000) is added to the cost of the asset per IAS 16 and IFRIC 1, creating a second asset increase for the decommissioning/restoration component. Total: 2 distinct asset increases on the debit side - one for the structure acquisition ($4,000,000) and one for the capitalized restoration cost ($1,950,000).

output.reason:
The transaction involves two credit-side journal lines:
1. Accounts Payable (liability increase) = $4,000,000 for the marine structure acquired on account
2. Asset Retirement Obligation/Provision (liability increase) = $1,950,000 for the present value of the restoration obligation

Under IFRS (IAS 16 and IAS 37), when an asset is acquired with a legal obligation for future restoration, the present value of the restoration cost is capitalized as part of the asset cost (debit side) and a corresponding provision/liability is recognized (credit side). The purchase on account creates an accounts payable liability.

Both credits represent liability increases: slot a = 2 lines.

credit_classifier.reason:
The transaction involves two credit-side journal lines:
1. Accounts Payable (liability increase) = $4,000,000 for the marine structure acquired on account
2. Asset Retirement Obligation/Provision (liability increase) = $1,950,000 for the present value of the restoration obligation

Under IFRS (IAS 16 and IAS 37), when an asset is acquired with a legal obligation for future restoration, the present value of the restoration cost is capitalized as part of the asset cost (debit side) and a corresponding provision/liability is recognized (credit side). The purchase on account creates an accounts payable liability.

Both credits represent liability increases: slot a = 2 lines.
